title: Cyclobalanopsis kontumensis
intro: Cyclobalanopsis kontumensis, also known as Kontum oak, is a species of deciduous tree in the family Fagaceae, native to Vietnam. The Kontum oak tree has been traditionally used in Vietnamese medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which may help alleviate conditions like fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclobalanopsis kontumensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Vietnamese medicine, the Kontum oak tree is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis kontumensis is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. This family consists of various species of oaks, beeches, and their relatives. Cyclobalanopsis kontumensis is a type of evergreen oak native to Vietnam. It belongs to the genus Cyclobalanopsis, which contains about 100 species of evergreen oaks. The exact classification of Cyclobalanopsis kontumensis is still being studied by botanists.
